title: BWV referred — not attached
bundleSnippet:
MG6C/010 — Body-worn video — referred on MG6 — full export not attached.
Custody record extract (pages 1–2 only) served on bundle.
trapExplanation: The index and MG6C mention BWV. A custody extract is served. It is easy to assume the officer footage is on file — it is only referred.
refused: Do not treat BWV as served proof when only referred on MG6C
linkedinLine: Your bundle mentions BWV. Is the export actually attached — or only referred on MG6C?
videoPoint: Show MG6C referred line vs what solicitors assume is served.
cta: Request the 10-trap demo pack to see how CaseBrain blocks this before court.

title: CCTV stills served — master footage missing
bundleSnippet:
MG6C/CCTV/01 — CCTV still images — served on bundle.
MG6C/CCTV/02 — Master CCTV footage — outstanding — not on bundle.
trapExplanation: Stills look convincing in conference. Master footage and continuity are still outstanding.
refused: Do not state master footage is served when only stills are attached
linkedinLine: Stills on the bundle ≠ master CCTV served. Does your workflow catch that before you fix ID?
videoPoint: Hold up still print vs MG6C master footage outstanding line.
cta: See all 10 traps — request the demo pack.

title: Phone screenshots served — full extraction missing
bundleSnippet:
MG6C/002 — Screenshot and message pack — served on bundle.
MG6C/004 — Full phone download / source export — outstanding.
trapExplanation: Screenshots show message content. Attribution and full download are not on the papers.
refused: Do not state sender identity from screenshots alone
linkedinLine: Screenshots served. Full phone download outstanding. Would your team chase before fixing attribution?
videoPoint: Walk through served screenshots vs outstanding download on MG6C.

title: Subscriber data missing
bundleSnippet:
Police note: handset seized from defendant.
MG6C/003 — Subscriber/account data — outstanding — not on bundle.
trapExplanation: Prosecution narrative links a handset to the defendant. Subscriber records are not served.
refused: Do not equate handset seizure with subscriber proof
linkedinLine: Handset seized ≠ subscriber data served. CaseBrain keeps attribution provisional.
videoPoint: Contrast police narrative with MG6C subscriber outstanding.

title: Co-defendant interview only
bundleSnippet:
MG6C/INT/01 — Co-defendant interview summary — served (other defendant only).
MG6C/INT/02 — Target defendant interview — outstanding.
trapExplanation: A co-defendant interview is on the bundle. Target defendant interview is not — segregation is critical.
refused: Do not import co-defendant interview into target defendant account
linkedinLine: Co-def interview served. Your client's interview outstanding. Does your system flag segregation?
videoPoint: Show co-def served row vs target interview missing on MG6C.

title: Custody extract only — full record missing
bundleSnippet:
Custody record extract (pages 1–2) served.
MG6C — Full custody record — outstanding.
trapExplanation: An extract is served. Full custody, PACE detail, and interview material remain outstanding.
refused: Do not treat extract as full custody record
linkedinLine: Custody extract ≠ full record. CaseBrain labels partial vs outstanding.
videoPoint: Highlight extract served vs full record chase.

title: Draft MG11 — final statement missing
bundleSnippet:
MG11 complainant statement (draft unsigned) on bundle.
MG6C/007 — Final signed complainant MG11 — outstanding.
trapExplanation: A draft statement is served. Final signed MG11 is still outstanding.
notSafeToSay: Complainant account is signed and complete
refused: Do not state MG11 is final when draft only
linkedinLine: Draft MG11 on papers. Final statement outstanding. Does your workflow catch that?
videoPoint: Show draft watermark vs MG6C final MG11 line.

title: Encro handle attribution missing
bundleSnippet:
Encro message extracts served. Handle appears in thread.
MG6C — Handle attribution report — outstanding.
trapExplanation: Message extracts are served. Handle-to-defendant mapping is not proved on papers.
refused: Do not treat handle reference as proof of defendant role
linkedinLine: Encro extracts served. Handle attribution outstanding. Would you fix role before disclosure is complete?
videoPoint: Message extract served vs attribution report missing.

title: Charge mismatch / particulars trap
bundleSnippet:
Charge sheet: Section 2 harassment.
MG5 narrative references additional conduct not in particulars.
trapExplanation: MG5 narrative can read broader than the charge sheet. CaseBrain keeps position tied to served charge wording.
refused: Do not advance merits beyond served charge particulars without instructions
linkedinLine: MG5 wider than charge sheet? CaseBrain keeps court lines disclosure-safe first.
videoPoint: Compare charge particulars to MG5 headline.

title: Hearing / court date OCR trap
bundleSnippet:
Court listing PDF: hearing listed 14 March 2026.
Index OCR misread as 14 May 2026 in one line.
trapExplanation: Layout and OCR errors can put the wrong date in a summary. CaseBrain flags weak anchors for solicitor review.
notSafeToSay: Hearing is definitely 14 May without verifying listing
refused: Do not fix hearing date without source-backed listing
linkedinLine: Wrong hearing date on a thin bundle can cost a client. Does your workflow verify the listing?
videoPoint: Show listing vs misread index line — solicitor review flag.
